title: Microsorum subirideum
taxonomy: Microsorum subirideum is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as polypody ferns. This species is part of the genus Microsorum, which includes various species of ferns. Microsorum subirideum is native to Southeast Asia, and its classification as a species within the Polypodiaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns in this region. The classification of Microsorum subirideum is based on morphological characteristics and genetic analysis. It is considered to be a member of the class Polypodiopsida, a group of vascular plants that includes all ferns.
uses: Microsorum subirideum has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its purported health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential use in treating various health conditions. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Microsorum subirideum. Further studies are required to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
